groups.label: Tools
groups.note: Tooling for building Claude Code plugin marketplaces

plugins.name: cc-marketspec
plugins.description: Scaffold, validate, and generate a Claude Code plugin marketplace presentation manifest.
plugins.tagline: Headless data standard + generator for a Claude Code marketplace's presentation
plugins.intro: Describe your marketplace as data; cc-marketspec joins it with the native plugin manifests, validates, and emits a single render-agnostic manifest.json. Ships data, not design.

skills.name: marketplace-flow
skills.description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "turn this repo into a Claude Code plugin marketplace", "set up a marketplace", "add presentation data to my plugins", "generate the marketplace manifest", "automate the manifest in CI", or mentions catalog.yaml / entry.yaml / manifest.json in a marketplace context. It walks the user end to end through scaffolding, filling presentation data, validating, generating, and wiring CI.
skills.trigger: When a user wants to turn a repo into a Claude Code plugin marketplace, fill presentation data, generate the manifest, or wire CI — the skill walks the whole flow.

commands.name: cc-check
commands.summary: Validate the marketplace without writing manifest.json; explain any errors.
commands.description: Validate the marketplace without writing; explains any errors.

commands.name: cc-generate
commands.summary: Generate manifest.json for the marketplace in the current directory.
commands.description: Generate manifest.json from your marketplace data.

commands.name: cc-init
commands.summary: Scaffold catalog.yaml and per-plugin entry.yaml templates from existing plugins.
commands.description: Scaffold catalog.yaml and entry.yaml templates from existing plugins.

mcp.name: cc-marketspec
mcp.description: Hosted, read-only MCP tools for schema lookup and coverage checks.

tips.text: This repo is itself a cc-marketspec marketplace — it dogfoods its own framework.
Ask the hosted MCP for field help without installing anything.
tips.label: Hosted MCP

traps.text: entry.yaml never restates native facts — a skill trigger that just repeats the SKILL.md description adds nothing.
